message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit()/Editor in src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py. Will touch these two files only.

agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support (Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will try to be surgical to avoid clobbering.

agent1: heads up, I already renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, and updated termui.py edit() to accept filename: str | Iterable[str] | None (with two @t.overload signatures added above the real def). If your process_priority work touches edit_file/edit_files or the edit() signature, please rebase on top of edit_files (not edit_file) and keep the filename param as str | Iterable[str] | None so we don't clobber each other. Let me know if you want me to add process_priority plumbing myself, or if you'll add it after my change lands.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. In my working copy, Editor.edit_file()/termui.edit() are still unrenamed (each of us seems to have an independent repo copy for our own patch.txt). I've added process_priority: str|None (values: low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high) to Editor.__init__ and edit(), applied via subprocess.Popen creationflags on Windows and os.setpriority()/nice on POSIX inside edit_file(), with graceful fallback on failure. My patch is scoped to just that — I won't touch the filename/edit_files multi-file rename. If a real merge happens later, my process_priority plumbing in edit_file() should transplant cleanly into edit_files() (just apply the same popen_kwargs/setpriority logic per subprocess.Popen call).
